div1.entryFree:
Stream, vb. 1) intr. to flow like a current; used only figuratively: round about her tear-distained eye blue circles --ed, like rainbows in the sky: these watergalls in her dim element foretell new storms, Lucr. 1587 (cf. All's I, 3, 157). to imperial Love do my sighs s. All's II, 3, 82. her eyes in heaven would through the airy region s. so bright, Rom. II, 2, 21 (cf. the subst. in Mids. V, 279).
2) tr. to emit, to pour out: as they (thy wounds) s. forth thy blood, Caes. III, 1, 201. == to unfurl, to display fully in the wind: --ing the ensign of the Christian cross against black pagans, R2 IV, 94.
